name: Modernizing Medicine (ModMed) EHR API
what_it_does: Modernizing Medicine (ModMed) specialty-specific EHR platform REST API for clinical documentation, practice management, and patient engagement for dermatology, ophthalmology, orthopedics, gastroenterology, plastic surgery, and urology specialty practices. Enables AI agents to manage clinical documentation and SOAP note creation for specialty clinical workflow automation, handle appointment scheduling and patient intake for specialty practice scheduling automation, access diagnosis coding and CPT code suggestion for specialty revenue cycle automation, retrieve specialty-specific clinical templates and adaptive learning documentation for specialty workflow automation, manage patient portal and communication for patient engagement automation, handle ePrescribing and controlled substance prescription management for prescription workflow automation, access prior authorization and insurance eligibility for specialty payer automation, retrieve clinical quality measure reporting for MIPS/MACRA compliance automation, manage pathology and diagnostic image ordering for specialty ancillary workflow automation, and integrate ModMed with labs, imaging centers, and specialty-specific revenue cycle tools for specialty practice intelligence.
use_cases: Documenting specialty encounters from clinical AI agents
Scheduling patients from specialty practice automation agents
Coding diagnoses from specialty revenue cycle agents
Reporting quality measures from MIPS compliance agents
not_for: Primary care and family medicine EHR (use Epic, Athenahealth, or eClinicalWorks)
best_when: A specialty medical practice (dermatology, ophthalmology, orthopedics, GI, plastic surgery) wanting AI agents to automate clinical documentation, appointment scheduling, specialty-specific coding, and quality measure reporting within ModMed's specialty-built EHR platform.
avoid_when: HIPAA PHI ACCESS REQUIRES BAA AND MINIMUM NECESSARY: Automated access to patient clinical records and PHI via ModMed API requires HIPAA Business Associate Agreement and minimum necessary standard; automated data extraction for population health analytics or AI training without minimum necessary limitation creates HIPAA Privacy Rule violation. CONTROLLED SUBSTANCE EPRESCRIBING DEANONYMIZATION RISK: Automated ePrescribing of controlled substances via ModMed must comply with DEA EPCS (Electronic Prescribing of Controlled Substances) regulations including two-factor authentication; automated controlled substance prescription workflow without DEA EPCS compliant authentication creates DEA violation. SPECIALTY CODING ACCURACY FOR REIMBURSEMENT RISK: Automated CPT and diagnosis code suggestion via ModMed specialty templates must be validated by licensed clinical coder or provider before claim submission; automated code submission without provider attestation creates false claims act exposure for specialty practice billing.

auth.notes: ModMed uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes and SMART on FHIR for patient data access. REST API with JSON and FHIR R4. Boca Raton, Florida HQ. Founded 2010 by Daniel Cane and Michael Rivers. Private ($231M raised, Warburg Pincus). 35,000+ physician users across specialty practices. Specialty EHR focus: dermatology (#1 dermatology EHR), ophthalmology, orthopedics, GI, plastic surgery, urology. Adaptive Learning AI for clinical documentation. ModMed Telehealth. Competes with NexTech, EyeCare Leaders, and Greenway Health for specialty EHR.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription per provider per specialty
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per provider; specialty-specific pricing; practice management and EHR bundled; add-ons for telehealth, patient kiosk, and analytics
pricing.notes: Boca Raton FL. Private ($231M raised, Warburg Pincus). Founded 2010. 35,000+ physician users. Annual subscription per provider. Specialty EHR pricing varies by specialty.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; clinical data queries throttled by SMART on FHIR scope; webhooks for appointment and document events

known_agent_gotchas: SMART ON FHIR SCOPE FOR PHI ACCESS: ModMed FHIR R4 API uses SMART on FHIR scopes; automated PHI access must request minimum necessary scopes per HIPAA minimum necessary standard; requesting overly broad FHIR scopes (patient/*.read) instead of resource-specific scopes creates HIPAA Privacy Rule audit risk; use resource-specific scopes (patient/Appointment.read, patient/Condition.read)

SPECIALTY-SPECIFIC TEMPLATE CUSTOMIZATION PER PRACTICE: ModMed adaptive learning templates are customized per specialty and practice; automated clinical documentation workflows built on default templates may not match practice-specific documentation requirements; obtain practice-specific template configuration before building automated documentation agents

DEA EPCS TWO-FACTOR FOR CONTROLLED SUBSTANCES: Automated controlled substance prescribing via ModMed ePrescribing requires DEA EPCS-compliant two-factor authentication for each prescription signing event; automated bulk controlled substance prescription signing without DEA EPCS compliant MFA creates DEA violation; implement EPCS authentication gate for controlled substance prescription workflows

ONC CERTIFICATION SCOPE FOR INFORMATION BLOCKING: ModMed is ONC-ACB certified; automated data blocking, export restriction, or selective API access that prevents patient data access creates ONC information blocking violation under 21st Century Cures Act; ensure automated API workflows do not restrict patient-requested data access

MIPS QUALITY MEASURE REPORTING SUBMISSION WINDOW: Automated MIPS/MACRA quality measure reporting via ModMed must submit within CMS annual performance year reporting window (typically January-March); automated quality measure submission outside CMS window creates missed MIPS reporting and payment adjustment penalty

PATHOLOGY LAB RESULT ROUTING TIMELINESS: Automated pathology result receipt via ModMed lab integration must implement critical value notification (positive melanoma, cancer diagnosis); automated result routing without critical value escalation creates patient safety risk for missed critical findings
agent_readiness.security_notes: Specialty EHR. HIPAA, SOC2, ONC-ACB. OAuth2/SMART on FHIR. US. Patient PHI, clinical documentation, and specialty diagnostic data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Specialty EHR. HIPAA, SOC2. OAuth2/SMART on FHIR. US. Patient PHI, clinical, and specialty diagnostic data.
